zh.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza於大約1134年出生在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 薩莫拉時，她的父親Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio20歲，母親Sancha López21歲。她和Alfonso IX de León至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza于大约1134年出生在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 薩莫拉时，她的父亲Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio20岁，母亲Sancha López21岁。她和Alfonso IX de León至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Iñiguez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Miguel, Jesus, Rafael, Luis, Javier, Ramon, Alfredo, Carlos, Enrique, Felipe.
Spanish (Íñiguez): patronymic from the personal name Íñigo (see Inigo ).

variants.name: Inigo
meanings: Spanish (Íñigo): from the personal name Íñigo, a Castilianized form of Basque Eneko (recorded in classical times as Enneco). The name Íñigo was in the Middle Ages usually Latinized as Ignatius ; it was the original name of Saint Ignatius of Loyola (Iñigo Yáñez de Oñaz y Loyola).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mendoza
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Ramon, Pedro, Roberto, Jorge.  Portuguese Anatolio, Godofredo, Anabela, Catarina, Ligia, Marcio, Paulo, Sil.

Basque: habitational name from any of several places in the provinces of Araba/Álava and Biscay in the Basque Country called Mendoza, named with Basque mendi ‘mountain’ + otz ‘cold’ + the definite article -a.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Iberian surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Mendosa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Fernando, Pedro, Ramon, Adolfo, Alberto, Anselmo, Benito, Bernadino, Consuelo, Domitila.
Hispanic: altered form of Basque   Mendoza  .

variants.name: Mandoza
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Basque   Mendoza  .

nameMeanings.name: Ines
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Agnes  , now also used, usually without the accent, in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Agnes
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza was born about 1134, in Diócesis de Zamora, Spain, her father, Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio, was 20 and her mother, Sancha López, was 21. She had at least 1 daughter with Alfonso IX de León.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza est née environ 1134, à Diócesis de Zamora, Espagne, son père, Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Sancha López, avait 21 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Alfonso IX de León.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza um 1134 in Diócesis de Zamora, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio 20 und ihre Mutter Sancha López 21. Sie hatte mit Alfonso IX de León mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza è nata il circa 1134 nel Diócesis de Zamora, Spagna, sua padre, Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Sancha López, aveva 21 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Alfonso IX de León.

ja.Zamora, Leon, Spain: スペインディoセシス ド ザモーア
ja.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain: スペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレド
ja.lifeSummary: Ines Iñiguez  de Mendozaが約1134年にスペインディoセシス ド ザモーアで生まれた時，父Íñigo López II Señor de Llodioは20歳，母Sancha Lópezは21歳であった。彼女はAlfonso IX de Leónとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza(이)가 대략 1134년 스페인 디o스시스 데 자모라에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sancha López(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 Alfonso IX de León(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza nasceu aproximadamente 1134, em Diócesis de Zamora, Espanha, seu pai, Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Sancha López, tinha 21 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Alfonso IX de León.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza родилась около 1134 г. в местности диoсесис де замора, Испания, её отцу по имени Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Sancha López, 21. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Alfonso IX de León.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ines Iñiguez  de Mendoza nació aproximadamente 1134, en Diócesis de Zamora, España, su papá, Íñigo López II Señor de Llodio, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Sancha López, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Alfonso IX de León.
